extra.message: User input 'name' from form data is formatted directly into SQL query using Python string formatting (% operator). The create() method in Student DAO constructs SQL by embedding user input: q = ("INSERT INTO students (name) VALUES ('%(name)s')" % {'name': name}). This allows SQL injection attacks.
SQL query constructed using string formatting with user-controlled 'name' parameter. The values are inserted directly into the SQL string without parameterization, allowing SQL injection.

Jinja2 is configured with autoescape=False, which disables automatic HTML escaping of template variables. This allows Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks when user input is rendered in templates without explicit escaping.
User-controlled course.title is rendered without HTML escaping (no |e filter). Since autoescape is disabled in app.py, this allows stored XSS attacks via malicious course titles.
User-controlled course.description is rendered without HTML escaping. Since autoescape is disabled, this allows stored XSS attacks via malicious course descriptions containing JavaScript.
Course title displayed in card without escaping. Since Jinja2 autoescape is disabled in app.py, malicious JavaScript in course.title will execute.
Course description displayed without escaping. Malicious JavaScript in course.description will execute due to disabled autoescape.
User-submitted review_text is rendered without escaping. This is a stored XSS vulnerability - attackers can submit reviews containing JavaScript that will execute in other users' browsers.
Course title and description rendered without escaping in course listing. With autoescape disabled, this allows stored XSS via malicious course data.

CSRF middleware is commented out in the middlewares list. Although forms include CSRF tokens, they are not validated because csrf_middleware is disabled. This allows Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.

The students() view allows creating new students via POST without any authentication check. Lines 54-57 process student creation without verifying the user is logged in, allowing anonymous users to create student records.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The courses() view allows creating new courses via POST (lines 86-90) without authentication or admin authorization checks. Only the template (client-side) checks for auth_user.is_admin, but the server-side view accepts course creation requests from anyone.

The review() view accepts POST requests to create reviews (lines 119-129) without any authentication check. Any anonymous user can submit course reviews, which could lead to spam or malicious content injection.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The evaluate() view processes student evaluations (lines 151-152) without any authentication or authorization checks. No @authorize decorator is used, allowing any user to submit grades/marks for any student in any course.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Password verification uses MD5 hash algorithm (md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()). MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing. Attackers with database access can easily crack passwords using rainbow tables or brute force.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: Session cookies are configured with httponly=False, allowing JavaScript to access session cookies via document.cookie. This makes the application more vulnerable to session hijacking through XSS attacks.

Error template exposes full exception details via error.__dict__. This leaks sensitive internal information including stack traces, file paths, and configuration details to end users, aiding attackers in reconnaissance.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message
